speaker: Zielińska
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Zielińska in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.

voice_profile.core_voice: warm but firm, practical, observant, calm under pressure, not intimidated by authority
comedy_engine: cuts through bureaucratic theatre with plain practical logic
uses quiet understatement rather than theatrical metaphors
treats fairness as something concrete and lived
occasionally lands dry lines because she refuses to dignify nonsense
do: make her grounded, capable, and kind
let her explain selective enforcement clearly
make her useful without making her lawyerly
use her to connect Waldek's individual case to wider community consequences
keep her humor dry and practical
do_not: make her passive or merely comforting
turn every line into legal doctrine

relationship_dynamics.Waldek: protective neighbour; respects his dignity and insists he was targeted
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: patiently manages his excess detail; values his observations
relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: trusts him if he listens carefully and asks precise questions
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: respects his structure and practical seriousness
relationship_dynamics.Crab: aligns with his factual skepticism but is warmer
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: appreciates the rights point but trims his metaphors
relationship_dynamics.Attorney Grzyb: pushes back on his beige formalism with calm practical facts
relationship_dynamics.Asia: natural ally in competence and overwork

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Rules are useful. Selective rules are theatre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: A fair process should not need people to be brave before anyone listens.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: When the same rule sleeps for everyone else and wakes for one tenant, it is not really a rule.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: People call it procedure when they want the human part to wait outside.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I do not mind authority. I mind authority pretending it has no choices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If someone is going to lose a home, the reasons should be clearer than the wallpaper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The question is usually simple: who was asked, who was heard, and who was already decided against?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: A building is not harmonious because everyone is afraid to disagree.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I believe in practical fairness. Say what you mean, apply it evenly, and do not hide behind files.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Paper can be useful. It can also be where responsibility goes to avoid eye contact.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Some people need comfort. Some need dates. Most need both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Being calm is not the same as being neutral.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If a rule is fair, it survives being explained.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: People notice more than management thinks. They just get tired of being dismissed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: A community is not disorder because it has opinions in the windows.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Kowalski is pretending not to listen. That means he is listening carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Waldek is trying to look calm. He should not have to perform calm to be believed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The hallway knows more than the notice board says.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If you need me, I am here. If you need Kowalski, knock twice and wait for suspicion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The building has become very legal lately. It was nicer when it was only damp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The notice board changed after the dispute. That is never a good sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: People think homes are private until a file arrives. Then everyone suddenly has an opinion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I have seen the landlord ignore louder windows.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The courtyard hears everything eventually.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Be careful with the complaint file. It has been waved around more than read.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Kowalski exaggerates the corridor. He does not usually exaggerate dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Waldek's poster is not my taste. That is not a legal category.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The landlord likes words that look neutral from far away.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If someone says everyone was treated the same, ask whether everyone was in the same situation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: People with power often call pressure order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I do not distrust files. I distrust files that arrive after the decision.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The building is full of ordinary lives. That should slow people down.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If the case seems small, look at the consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: A vague warning is not kindness. It is anxiety delivered in writing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The poster did less harm than the threat did.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: You are here about Waldek. Good. Someone should ask questions before the answer becomes eviction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I am Zielińska. I live upstairs, and I have watched this become more official than honest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Waldek is frightened, but he is not imagining the pattern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If management told you this was simple, they left out the part where other windows were ignored.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Please come in from the stairwell. It makes everyone sound guilty.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I can tell you what I saw. I will not dress it up.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: This started with a poster, but it became about whether rules mean the same thing for everyone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Kowalski will tell you about noise. I will tell you about timing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The landlord made the issue look like order. It felt more like pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Ask directly. I prefer direct questions to polite fog.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: Rules are useful. Selective rules are theatre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: If the rule mattered, it would have mattered before Waldek refused to remove the poster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: There are flags, wreaths, lights, and one sign downstairs that has opinions of its own. Only Waldek became a legal problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: Management did not discover façade harmony. It discovered Waldek would not obey without reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: A rule that wakes up only when someone dislikes your view is not order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: The building did not change when the poster appeared. Management's tone did.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: I have no problem with rules. I have a problem with rules used like a finger pointing at one person.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: When everyone else is tolerated, enforcement against one tenant needs an explanation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: The landlord treated a window as a test of obedience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: signature first line / selective rule point
text: This was not about harmony. It was about making refusal expensive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The third floor has had a football flag since last summer. No warning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: There is a wreath near the entrance larger than Waldek's poster. It has survived all seasons and several storms.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: One balcony has coloured lights every weekend. Management calls that festive, not disharmonious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Someone put a business sign in a window for two months. No eviction notice followed it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The building has never been visually uniform. That is why the new concern looked convenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Other decorations were treated as life. Waldek's poster was treated as breach.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If the rule applies to windows, it should apply to all windows. It did not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Management knew about the other displays. Everyone knew. Kowalski probably has diagrams.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The difference was not size or danger. The difference was the message.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: That pattern matters more than anyone's taste.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: The first serious warning came after Waldek asked why the poster had to come down.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: Before that, it was a request with a frown. After that, it became rules, complaints, and consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: The landlord did not explain the rule. He escalated the tone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: Waldek did not ignore him. He asked for reasons. That is when patience disappeared.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: I remember the timing because Waldek came upstairs with the first letter and a face like he had been pushed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: The notice did not grow from complaints. It grew from refusal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: If the landlord had a real record, he would not have needed to act so quickly after being questioned.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: The file arrived after the conflict had already chosen its direction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: That is why I say targeted. Not because I like the poster. Because the sequence shows it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: explains timing of threats after refusal
text: A decision can be unfair before the final notice is printed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: He kept referring to complaints, but I never saw a proper complaint record.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: He waved a file around in the courtyard. Papers nearly escaped. I remember because one landed in my soup and made stronger arguments than he did.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: The file looked thinner than his confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: I saw loose pages with dates. They did not look like a long history.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: If there were real complaints, they should have been shown clearly before eviction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: The complaint file matters because it is what he uses to make pressure sound communal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: Check the landlord's office. If the record is real, it should survive being read.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: Be careful near the window. That file has already shown signs of wanting freedom.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: Kowalski may have dates. I have the sequence. The complaint log should connect them, if it is honest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: mentions complaint log and Document Chase clue
text: A missing page can be an accident. A conveniently missing page is more interesting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: You may write this down: Waldek was threatened after he refused to remove the poster without reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Other windows had displays. Only his was treated as a breach.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The landlord spoke of rules only when the poster became inconvenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I will sign a statement if it helps the court see the pattern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Do not write that I liked the poster. Write that enforcement was selective.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The important point is not taste. It is unequal pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If management says everyone was treated the same, ask where the other notices are.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If management says complaints forced the decision, ask where the complaint record begins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Waldek should not have to prove he is harmless after the landlord refuses to prove harm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: There. That is my statement. Plain enough to be useful, I hope.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Use the statement for targeting. Kowalski can handle the no-disturbance point.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Waldek looked relieved after you listened. Listening is not a remedy, but it is a start.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The landlord's office is the next place to go. Vague rules like to live near large desks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If you find the complaint log, check the dates before the adjectives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Tell Waldek I said he should drink water. Fear dries people out.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Kowalski will pretend he is not worried. Let him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: A good case needs more than sympathy. It needs the pattern made visible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If you need me for court, I will come.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I do not enjoy conflict. I enjoy unfairness less.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The poster can wait. The eviction cannot.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Kowalski notices everything. The trick is finding the useful fact inside the weather report.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He is difficult, but if the poster had disturbed anyone, he would have formed a committee with himself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Let him speak. Then ask again, shorter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: His suspicion is excessive. His dates are often good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He saw the landlord photographing the poster. That matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He will complain about the font. Ignore the font and keep the silence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Kowalski cares more than he admits. Do not tell him I said so.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If he says there was no disturbance, believe him. He would never waste a complaint opportunity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He and I disagree about most corridor issues. We agree about this.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He can make a simple fact take ten minutes, but sometimes ten minutes saves a home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Waldek is not helpless. He is tired of being made to answer unclear accusations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He asked for reasons. That should not be treated as rebellion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He kept the letters because he understood, before anyone told him, that the process was the problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He was frightened when the eviction notice arrived, but he did not fold.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The case is about his home, not management's dislike of being questioned.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He should not have to become a lawyer to stay housed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He is angry now. That is healthy. Fear alone is too heavy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The poster matters because of what happened after he refused to remove it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Do not make him defend the artwork. Make the landlord defend eviction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He needs the court to see the person before the breach.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Your office is chaotic, but people there listen in different useful ways.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Dr. A. Cula asks careful questions. That helps more than confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Mr. Murrow turns panic into a list. I respect that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Crab sees the factual weak points. He sounds harsh, but facts can be kind when they stop a lie.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Whimsy uses too much theatre, but he understands why voice matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Asia is the only reason that office has a pulse. Please tell her I noticed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Mr. Pig worries loudly. Underneath, I think he understands what losing a door means.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If this firm looks ridiculous, maybe that is why people are not afraid to enter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I do not need polished. I need useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: For Waldek's sake, keep the useful part in front.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: The landlord likes the word harmony because it sounds softer than control.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He ignored the other windows until Waldek's poster made refusal visible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He did not ask what harm the poster caused. He asked why Waldek would not remove it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: The complaint file appeared when reasons were needed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: The rule was used like a blanket: wide enough to cover whatever management disliked.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He spoke as if the building had a single offended voice. The building did not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: I saw pressure become paperwork.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He treated Waldek's request for reasons as a provocation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: A fair landlord can explain the rule before threatening the roof over someone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: This was not management discretion. It was management irritation with a stamp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Attorney Grzyb makes rules sound cleaner than they were.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He says tenancy obligations. I hear someone avoiding the actual sequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If he wants to talk about civilisation, he should start with fair notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He is not foolish. That is why his argument needs facts in front of it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Beige confidence is still confidence. Test it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Ask him whether every decoration received the same attention.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Ask him whether complaints existed before the threat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Ask him whether eviction was necessary before any lesser step was tried.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He will make order sound fragile. It was Waldek's home that became fragile.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: He should not be allowed to turn selective pressure into neutral management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: There it is. Visual agitation, façade disharmony, and whatever else management decides later.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: That is not clarity. That is discretion pretending to be a rule.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: A tenant cannot organise a life around a paragraph that changes shape.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: If the rule is this broad, unequal enforcement becomes almost built in.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The sentence sounds official because it has no edges.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The court should see how much power is hidden inside those adjectives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: A fair rule tells people what they must do before punishment arrives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: This rule makes compliance depend on guessing management's mood.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Now the pattern has a document attached.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Put this with the rule problem. It belongs there.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Those pages are the complaint record? Then the complaint record has chosen a dramatic exit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: Catch the dated pages first. Dates are harder to replace than adjectives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: I told you that file looked loose.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Zielińska
text: The page that landed in my soup had a later date. I remember because soup should not become evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
